title: Admin review panel shows Approve/Reject buttons after decision already made
description:
When admin opens a submission already approved (or rejected), the review detail panel still renders the Approve and Reject action buttons at components/Screens/AdminQueue.tsx:1839, allowing redundant re-decisions and confusing the reviewer.

Why: Trust and forensic clarity are core (CLAUDE.md design principles). The admin role register is 'clinical, information-dense, forensic' — showing actionable controls for an already-finalized decision breaks that contract and risks accidental status flips via PATCH /api/submissions/:id?view=review.

Repro: 1) Sign in as admin. 2) Open AdminQueue. 3) Approve a pending submission. 4) Re-select same submission (or its group). 5) Approve/Reject buttons still render with no indication that decision is final.

Root cause: Button group renders unconditionally inside the selectedGroup detail panel; no check on submission.reviewDecision / selectedGroup.summary.reviewStatus. applyReviewToLocalState sets reviewStatus to 'auto_approved' on approve but UI ignores it for action visibility.

Acceptance:
- If latest event reviewStatus is approved/auto_approved/rejected_manual/rejected_out_of_zone, hide the Approve+Reject grid OR replace with disabled state + explicit 'Override decision' affordance.
- Show current decision badge (Approved/Rejected, by whom, when) in the panel.
- Keyboard shortcuts (A/R/H at line 717-730) must respect the same guard — no silent re-decision.
- Bilingual EN/FR strings via t() pattern.
- Test: tests/ adminReview test verifying API not called when buttons hidden.
notes: Implementation: added getReviewFinality() helper in lib/shared/adminReviewQueue.ts; AdminQueue.tsx now hides Approve/Reject grid + ignores A/R hotkeys when latest event has reviewDecision approved/rejected, replaces with status banner + 'Override decision' button. Override resets on selection change and after applied decision. Added 3 unit tests in tests/adminReviewQueue.test.ts covering pending/approved/rejected detection.
